prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap of pyridine, energy difference in eV.
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis set (tier "standard"), density-fitted, as implemented in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the fixed Cartesian coordinates provided in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz (no re-optimization; thus DFT//input-XYZ).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet, RHF/RKS reference).
Convergence & warnings: SCF converged in 10 cycles; no warnings were reported by the engine. Integrity check status: ok (trustworthy = true).
Live log: the PySCF stdout/stderr stream was written live to the autogenerated frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory (same directory where the command was executed). Consult that file for full SCF details and reproducibility metadata. Result JSON saved to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpgcxqo5n6/live.json via --out flag; see its cli_invocation field for the exact command line.

Result
HOMO energy = −7.2525992 eV
LUMO energy = −1.112786 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.13981314638125 eV (Koopmans-type orbital gap, not an excitation energy).

No warnings were produced during the calculation.

All data above were generated by chemkit’s frontier-orbitals skill calling PySCF (GFN2-xTB was not used). Calculated values are provided as-is; no experimental comparison requested.
